-## What Still Needs To Change
+## Multi-Channel Preferences Update (Session 9)
 
-### User Preferences Model (`src/models/user-preferences.ts`)
+### Decision Change
 
-The preferences model was designed with an **email-only assumption**. The key assumption that has changed:
+> **Old assumption (Session 3):** "Currently focused on email settings since email is the only notification channel."
+>
+> **New reality (Session 8+9):** Email, SMS, and Webhook are all supported channels. The preferences model now supports per-channel settings.
 
-> **Old assumption:** "Currently focused on email settings since email is the only notification channel."
-
-This is **no longer valid**. SMS is now a supported channel.
-
-#### Required Changes
+### Changes Made
 
 | # | File | Change |
 |---|------|--------|
-| 1 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `SmsPreferences` interface (similar to `EmailPreferences` but without `frequency`) |
-| 2 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Add `sms: SmsPreferences` field to `UserNotificationPreferences` |

-| 8 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `createPreferences()` to handle `sms` field |
-| 9 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Update `updatePreferences()` to handle `sms` field |
-| 10 | `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` | Add tests for SMS preference checks |
-
-### Suggested `SmsPreferences` Interface
-
-```typescript
-interface SmsPreferences {
-  /** Master switch: if false, no SMS notifications are sent. */
-  enabled: boolean;
-  /** Per-category preferences. Categories not listed default to enabled. */
-  categoryPreferences: NotificationCategoryPreference[];
-}
-```
-
-> **Note:** SMS does not need a `frequency` field because SMS is always delivered immediately (no batching/digest support).
-
-### Consider Generalising
-
-With two channels now and more planned (webhook, in_app), consider whether the preferences model should be generalised to a `Record<NotificationChannel, ChannelPreferences>` pattern instead of adding named fields for each channel. This would make adding future channels easier but is a larger refactor.
-
----
-
-## Current Behaviour (Before Preferences Update)
-
-- SMS notifications are **always sent** to users, even if they have stored preferences
-- The `PreferencesService.shouldSendNotification()` method returns `true` for the `'sms'` channel because it falls through to the "unknown channel" default
-- This is **safe** (no notifications are lost) but means **users cannot opt out of SMS yet**

+### Default Preferences
+
+- **Email:** Enabled by default, immediate frequency, all categories enabled
+- **SMS:** Enabled by default, all categories enabled (always immediate)
+- **Webhook:** Disabled by default (requires URL configuration), all categories enabled
+
+### Backward Compatibility
+
+- The `email` field on `UserNotificationPreferences` is unchanged in structure
+- `CreatePreferencesInput` and `UpdatePreferencesInput` still accept `email`-only input — `sms` and `webhook` fields are optional and default to sensible values
+- The `NotificationService` continues to work without a `PreferencesService` (all notifications sent)
+- Existing code that only uses email preferences continues to work without modification
 
 ---
 
-## How to Register the SMS Adapter
+## How to Register Adapters
 
 ```typescript
-import { EventBus, NotificationService, SmsAdapter } from './src/index.js';
+import {
+  EventBus,
+  NotificationService,
+  PreferencesService,
+  PreferencesRepository,
+  Database,
+  EmailAdapter,
+  SmsAdapter,
+  WebhookAdapter,
+} from './src/index.js';
 
 const eventBus = new EventBus();
 const notificationService = new NotificationService(eventBus);
 
-// Register the SMS adapter
-const smsAdapter = new SmsAdapter({ stubMode: true });
-notificationService.registerAdapter(smsAdapter);
+// Register adapters for each channel
+notificationService.registerAdapter(new EmailAdapter({ stubMode: true }));
+notificationService.registerAdapter(new SmsAdapter({ stubMode: true }));
+notificationService.registerAdapter(new WebhookAdapter({
+  url: 'https://hooks.example.com',
+  stubMode: true,
+}));
+
+// Wire up preferences service for per-channel opt-out
+const db = new Database();
+const prefsRepo = new PreferencesRepository(db);
+const prefsService = new PreferencesService(prefsRepo);
+notificationService.setPreferencesService(prefsService);
 ```
 
-The notification service will then route any notification with `channel: 'sms'` to the SMS adapter.
+The notification service will check per-channel preferences before dispatching to each adapter.

index 0000000..bd06bf4
--- /dev/null
+++ b/dist/adapters/sms.adapter.d.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,187 @@
+/**
+ * SMS notification adapter — delivers notifications via SMS.
+ *
+ * This is a fully implemented SMS adapter with:
+ * - Phone number validation: Validates recipient phone number format before sending.
+ * - Message formatting: Converts notification payloads to concise SMS-friendly
+ *   text messages with character-aware truncation.
+ * - Configurable delivery: Stub mode for development/testing, production
+ *   mode for real SMS API integration.
+ *
+ * Implements the NotificationAdapter interface. Registered with the
+ * NotificationService to handle all notifications on the 'sms' channel.
+ *
+ * Configuration is injected via constructor to support different
+ * environments (dev, staging, production).
+ *
+ * NOTE: Adding SMS as a notification channel means the user preferences model
+ * (src/models/user-preferences.ts) needs to be updated. The current model was
+ * designed with an email-only assumption — see SMS_CHANNEL_NOTES.md for details.
+ */
+import type { NotificationAdapter } from './notification.adapter.js';
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationType } from '../models/notification.js';
+/**
+ * A formatted SMS message ready for delivery.
+ */
+export interface SmsMessage {
+    /** Sender phone number or short code. */
+    from: string;
+    /** Recipient phone number. */
+    to: string;
+    /** SMS message body (plain text, max 160 chars for single segment). */
+    body: string;
+    /** When the SMS was composed. */
+    composedAt: Date;
+    /** The notification type that generated this SMS. */
+    notificationType: NotificationType;
+    /** Additional headers or metadata. */
+    metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+/**
+ * Configuration for the SMS adapter.
+ */
+export interface SmsAdapterConfig {
+    /** Sender phone number or short code (e.g., '+15551234567'). */
+    fromNumber: string;
+    /** Whether this is a stub (logs instead of sending). */
+    stubMode: boolean;
+    /** Maximum retries for failed delivery attempts. */
+    maxRetries: number;
+    /** Maximum characters per SMS message. Messages longer than this are truncated. */
+    maxMessageLength: number;
+}
+/**
+ * Default configuration for development/testing.
+ */
+export declare const defaultSmsConfig: SmsAdapterConfig;
+/**
+ * Validates a phone number format.
+ *
+ * Accepts E.164 format phone numbers (international standard):
+ * - Must start with '+'
+ * - Followed by 1-3 digit country code
+ * - Followed by subscriber number (total 8-15 digits after '+')
+ *
+ * Also accepts common domestic formats:
+ * - 10-digit numbers (e.g., '5551234567')
+ * - Formatted numbers (e.g., '(555) 123-4567', '555-123-4567')
+ *
+ * @param phoneNumber - The phone number to validate.
+ * @returns true if the phone number format is valid.
+ */
+export declare function isValidPhoneNumber(phoneNumber: string): boolean;
+/**
+ * Normalises a phone number to a consistent format.
+ *
+ * Strips formatting characters and trims whitespace. Returns the
+ * normalised number or null if the input is empty or invalid.
+ *
+ * @param phoneNumber - The phone number to normalise.
+ * @returns An object with the normalised number and validity, or null if empty.
+ */
+export declare function resolvePhoneNumber(phoneNumber: string): {
+    number: string;
+    isValid: boolean;
+} | null;
+/**
+ * Formats a notification payload as a concise SMS message body.
+ *
+ * SMS messages are limited in length, so this function creates a compact
+ * representation of the notification. If the formatted message exceeds
+ * the maximum length, it is truncated with an ellipsis.
+ *
+ * @param payload - The notification payload to format.
+ * @param maxLength - Maximum message length (default: 160).
+ * @returns A formatted SMS message body string.
+ */
+export declare function formatSmsMessage(payload: NotificationPayload, maxLength?: number): string;
+/**
+ * SMS notification adapter.
+ *
+ * Sends notifications via SMS with phone number validation and message
+ * formatting. In stub mode (default), logs the SMS details and records
+ * them in memory instead of actually delivering.
+ *
+ * Pipeline:
+ * 1. Validate recipient phone number format.
+ * 2. Format notification into a concise SMS message body.
+ * 3. Compose complete SmsMessage (from, to, body).
+ * 4. Deliver via stub (log + record) or production (SMS API).
+ * 5. Return success/failure result (never throws).
+ */
+export declare class SmsAdapter implements NotificationAdapter {
+    readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+    private logger;
+    private config;
+    private sentMessages;
+    constructor(config?: Partial<SmsAdapterConfig>);
+    /**
+     * Send a notification via SMS.
+     *
+     * Full pipeline:
+     * 1. Validates the recipient phone number.
+     * 2. Formats the notification as a concise SMS message.
+     * 3. Composes the complete SMS message.
+     * 4. Delivers via stub (records in memory) or production mode.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to deliver via SMS.
+     * @returns Result indicating success or failure. Never throws.
+     */
+    send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+    /**
+     * Get all SMS messages sent in stub mode. Useful for testing.
+     *
+     * Returns defensive copies of the recorded SMS messages.
+     */
+    getSentMessages(): SmsMessage[];
+    /**
+     * Clear sent SMS records. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    clearSentMessages(): void;
+    /**
+     * Get the current adapter configuration.
+     */
+    getConfig(): Readonly<SmsAdapterConfig>;
+    /**
+     * Format a message for a given payload without sending.
+     * Useful for previewing SMS messages or testing formatting in isolation.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to format.
+     * @returns The formatted SMS message body string.
+     */
+    formatMessage(payload: NotificationPayload): string;
+    /**
+     * Validate a recipient phone number without sending.
+     *
+     * @param phoneNumber - The phone number to validate.
+     * @returns true if the phone number format is valid.
+     */
+    validateRecipient(phoneNumber: string): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Attempt SMS delivery with retry logic.
+     *
+     * Retries up to `config.maxRetries` times on transient failures.
+     * Throws on permanent failure after all retries are exhausted.
+     *
+     * @param sms - The complete SMS message to deliver.
+     * @throws Error if delivery fails after all retries.
+     */
+    private deliverWithRetry;
+    /**
+     * Real SMS delivery — override in production or replace with actual SMS API.
+     *
+     * In a real deployment, this method would:
+     * - Connect to an SMS provider API (e.g., Twilio, AWS SNS, Vonage)
+     * - Authenticate with API credentials
+     * - Send the SMS message
+     * - Parse and return the provider response
+     *
+     * @param _sms - The SMS message to deliver.
+     * @throws Error if delivery fails.
+     */
+    private deliverSms;
+    /**
+     * Simple delay utility for retry backoff.
+     */
+    private delay;
+}

+/**
+ * Webhook notification adapter — delivers notifications via HTTP POST.
+ *
+ * This is a fully implemented webhook adapter with:
+ * - URL validation: Validates the webhook URL format before sending.
+ * - JSON payload formatting: Converts notification payloads to structured
+ *   JSON bodies with all relevant fields and timestamps.
+ * - Configurable delivery: Stub mode for development/testing, production
+ *   mode for real HTTP delivery.
+ *
+ * Implements the NotificationAdapter interface. Registered with the
+ * NotificationService to handle all notifications on the 'webhook' channel.
+ *
+ * Configuration is injected via constructor to support different
+ * environments (dev, staging, production).
+ */
+import type { NotificationAdapter } from './notification.adapter.js';
+import type { NotificationChannel, NotificationPayload, NotificationResult, NotificationType } from '../models/notification.js';
+/**
+ * The JSON body sent to a webhook endpoint.
+ */
+export interface WebhookPayloadBody {
+    /** The notification type that generated this webhook call. */
+    notificationType: NotificationType;
+    /** Who receives the notification. */
+    recipient: string;
+    /** Subject line or title. */
+    subject: string;
+    /** Notification body content. */
+    body: string;
+    /** The delivery channel (always 'webhook'). */
+    channel: 'webhook';
+    /** Delivery priority. */
+    priority: string;
+    /** When the webhook payload was composed. */
+    timestamp: string;
+    /** Optional key-value metadata for adapter-specific data. */
+    metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+/**
+ * A complete webhook delivery record for testing and auditing.
+ */
+export interface WebhookDeliveryRecord {
+    /** The URL the webhook was sent to. */
+    url: string;
+    /** The JSON body that was POSTed. */
+    payload: WebhookPayloadBody;
+    /** When the delivery was initiated. */
+    deliveredAt: Date;
+    /** The notification type that triggered this webhook. */
+    notificationType: NotificationType;
+}
+/**
+ * Configuration for the webhook adapter.
+ */
+export interface WebhookAdapterConfig {
+    /** The URL to POST webhook notifications to. */
+    url: string;
+    /** Whether this is a stub (logs instead of sending). */
+    stubMode: boolean;
+    /** Maximum retries for failed delivery attempts. */
+    maxRetries: number;
+    /** Request timeout in milliseconds. */
+    timeoutMs: number;
+    /** Optional secret for signing webhook payloads. */
+    secret?: string;
+}
+/**
+ * Default configuration for development/testing.
+ */
+export declare const defaultWebhookConfig: WebhookAdapterConfig;
+/**
+ * Validates a webhook URL format.
+ *
+ * Checks:
+ * - Is a non-empty string
+ * - Starts with http:// or https://
+ * - Can be parsed by the URL constructor
+ *
+ * @param url - The webhook URL to validate.
+ * @returns true if the URL format is valid.
+ */
+export declare function isValidWebhookUrl(url: string): boolean;
+/**
+ * Formats a notification payload as a JSON webhook body.
+ *
+ * Creates a structured JSON object suitable for POSTing to a webhook
+ * endpoint. Includes all relevant notification fields plus a timestamp.
+ *
+ * @param payload - The notification payload to format.
+ * @returns A structured webhook payload body.
+ */
+export declare function formatWebhookPayload(payload: NotificationPayload): WebhookPayloadBody;
+/**
+ * Webhook notification adapter.
+ *
+ * Sends notifications via HTTP POST with JSON payloads. In stub mode
+ * (default), logs the webhook details and records them in memory instead
+ * of actually delivering.
+ *
+ * Pipeline:
+ * 1. Validate webhook URL configuration.
+ * 2. Format notification into a JSON webhook payload.
+ * 3. Deliver via stub (log + record) or production (HTTP POST).
+ * 4. Return success/failure result (never throws).
+ */
+export declare class WebhookAdapter implements NotificationAdapter {
+    readonly channel: NotificationChannel;
+    private logger;
+    private config;
+    private deliveries;
+    constructor(config?: Partial<WebhookAdapterConfig>);
+    /**
+     * Send a notification via webhook.
+     *
+     * Full pipeline:
+     * 1. Validates the webhook URL.
+     * 2. Formats the notification as a JSON payload.
+     * 3. Delivers via stub (records in memory) or production mode.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to deliver via webhook.
+     * @returns Result indicating success or failure. Never throws.
+     */
+    send(payload: NotificationPayload): Promise<NotificationResult>;
+    /**
+     * Get all webhook deliveries in stub mode. Useful for testing.
+     *
+     * Returns defensive copies of the recorded webhook delivery records,
+     * including the JSON payload that was (or would have been) sent.
+     */
+    getDeliveries(): WebhookDeliveryRecord[];
+    /**
+     * Clear delivery records. Useful for testing.
+     */
+    clearDeliveries(): void;
+    /**
+     * Get the current adapter configuration.
+     */
+    getConfig(): Readonly<WebhookAdapterConfig>;
+    /**
+     * Format a payload without sending. Useful for previewing webhook
+     * bodies or testing formatting in isolation.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The notification payload to format.
+     * @returns The formatted webhook payload body.
+     */
+    formatPayload(payload: NotificationPayload): WebhookPayloadBody;
+    /**
+     * Validate the configured webhook URL without sending.
+     *
+     * @returns true if the configured URL is valid.
+     */
+    validateUrl(): boolean;
+    /**
+     * Attempt webhook delivery with retry logic.
+     *
+     * Retries up to `config.maxRetries` times on transient failures.
+     * Throws on permanent failure after all retries are exhausted.
+     *
+     * @param payload - The webhook payload body to POST.
+     * @throws Error if delivery fails after all retries.
+     */
+    private deliverWithRetry;
+    /**
+     * Real webhook delivery — override in production or replace with actual HTTP client.
+     *
+     * In a real deployment, this method would:
+     * - Create an HTTP POST request to `config.url`
+     * - Set Content-Type to application/json
+     * - Include the JSON payload in the request body
+     * - Optionally sign the payload with `config.secret`
+     * - Respect the `config.timeoutMs` timeout
+     *
+     * @param _payload - The webhook payload to deliver.
+     * @throws Error if delivery fails.
+     */
+    private deliverWebhook;
+    /**
+     * Simple delay utility for retry backoff.
+     */
+    private delay;
+}

+/**
+ * Notification log model — types for tracking notification delivery history.
+ *
+ * Architecture: The NotificationHistoryService listens for notification lifecycle
+ * events (notification:sent, notification:failed) on the EventBus and persists
+ * a NotificationLogEntry for each via the NotificationLogRepository.
+ *
+ * This provides a durable, queryable audit trail of all notification activity,
+ * separate from the in-memory records held by NotificationService.
+ *
+ * Fields captured per entry:
+ * - recipient: Who the notification was sent to
+ * - channel: The delivery channel used (email, sms, webhook, in_app)
+ * - type: The notification type (order_confirmation, welcome, etc.)
+ * - timestamp: When the log entry was recorded
+ * - deliveryStatus: Whether delivery succeeded or failed
+ * - notificationId: Link back to the original NotificationRecord ID
+ * - subject/body: Content that was sent
+ * - failureReason: If delivery failed, why
+ * - metadata: Any additional context carried from the notification
+ */
+import type { NotificationType, NotificationChannel, NotificationPriority } from './notification.js';
+/**
+ * Delivery status for a logged notification.
+ */
+export type DeliveryStatus = 'sent' | 'failed';
+/**
+ * A persisted log entry for a notification that was dispatched.
+ * Created by the NotificationHistoryService when it observes
+ * notification:sent or notification:failed events.
+ */
+export interface NotificationLogEntry {
+    /** Unique log entry ID (e.g., nlog-0001). */
+    id: string;
+    /** The original NotificationRecord ID this log entry corresponds to. */
+    notificationId: string;
+    /** The notification type. */
+    type: NotificationType;
+    /** The delivery channel used. */
+    channel: NotificationChannel;
+    /** Recipient identifier (email, user ID, webhook URL, etc.). */
+    recipient: string;
+    /** Subject line of the notification. */
+    subject: string;
+    /** Body content of the notification. */
+    body: string;
+    /** Delivery priority. */
+    priority: NotificationPriority;
+    /** Whether delivery succeeded or failed. */
+    deliveryStatus: DeliveryStatus;
+    /** When the log entry was recorded. */
+    timestamp: Date;
+    /** Reason for failure (if deliveryStatus is 'failed'). */
+    failureReason?: string;
+    /** Optional metadata carried from the notification. */
+    metadata?: Record<string, unknown>;
+}
+/**
+ * Query options for filtering notification history.
+ * All fields are optional; omit a field to skip that filter.
+ */
+export interface NotificationHistoryQuery {
+    /** Filter by recipient identifier. */
+    recipient?: string;
+    /** Filter by notification type. */
+    type?: NotificationType;
+    /** Filter by delivery channel. */
+    channel?: NotificationChannel;
+    /** Filter by delivery status. */
+    deliveryStatus?: DeliveryStatus;
+    /** Include only entries on or after this date. */
+    startDate?: Date;
+    /** Include only entries on or before this date. */
+    endDate?: Date;
+}

+/**
+ * Notification history service — persists and queries notification delivery history.
+ *
+ * ARCHITECTURE DECISION: Event-Driven Log Capture
+ * ================================================
+ * This service integrates with the existing notification flow by subscribing
+ * to notification lifecycle events on the EventBus:
+ * - notification:sent  → logs a successful delivery
+ * - notification:failed → logs a failed delivery
+ *
+ * It does NOT intercept or modify the notification dispatch path. Instead it
+ * passively observes the events that the NotificationService already emits,
+ * making it fully decoupled and backwards-compatible.
+ *
+ * The logged entries are persisted through the NotificationLogRepository
+ * (Repository pattern), providing a durable, queryable audit trail.
+ *
+ * Query capabilities:
+ * - By recipient (who received the notification)
+ * - By notification type (order_confirmation, welcome, etc.)
+ * - By delivery channel (email, sms, webhook, in_app)
+ * - By delivery status (sent, failed)
+ * - By date range (start/end timestamps)
+ * - Combined filtering via NotificationHistoryQuery
+ * - Paginated results via the paginate() utility
+ *
+ * Depends on:
+ * - EventBus (event system) for receiving notification lifecycle events
+ * - NotificationLogRepository (data layer) for persistence
+ *
+ * CONSTRAINTS:
+ * - All event subscriptions go through EventBus.on() (no CallbackRegistry).
+ * - This service is read-heavy; writes only happen in response to events.
+ * - Does NOT depend on NotificationService directly (loose coupling via events).
+ */
+import type { EventBus } from '../events/event-bus.js';
+import type { NotificationLogRepository } from '../repositories/notification-log.repository.js';
+import type { NotificationLogEntry, NotificationHistoryQuery } from '../models/notification-log.js';
+import type { NotificationType, NotificationChannel } from '../models/notification.js';
+import type { PaginationResult } from '../utils/pagination.js';
+/** Reset the ID counter. Used in tests. */
+export declare function resetNotificationLogIdCounter(): void;
+export declare class NotificationHistoryService {
+    private eventBus;
+    private logRepository;
+    private logger;
+    constructor(eventBus: EventBus, logRepository: NotificationLogRepository);
+    /**
+     * Register event handlers for notification lifecycle events.
+     *
+     * IMPORTANT: ALL event subscriptions go through EventBus.on().
+     * Do NOT use CallbackRegistry or direct callbacks.
+     */
+    private registerEventHandlers;
+    /**
+     * Handle a successful notification delivery — log it as 'sent'.
+     */
+    private handleNotificationSent;
+    /**
+     * Handle a failed notification delivery — log it as 'failed'.
+     */
+    private handleNotificationFailed;
+    /**
+     * Get all notification log entries.
+     */
+    getAll(): NotificationLogEntry[];
+    /**
+     * Get the total count of log entries.
+     */
+    getCount(): number;
+    /**
+     * Get a specific log entry by its ID.
+     */
+    getById(id: string): NotificationLogEntry | undefined;
+    /**
+     * Get the log entry for a specific notification ID.
+     */
+    getByNotificationId(notificationId: string): NotificationLogEntry | undefined;
+    /**
+     * Get all log entries for a specific recipient.
+     *
+     * @param recipient - The recipient identifier to filter by.
+     */
+    getByRecipient(recipient: string): NotificationLogEntry[];
+    /**
+     * Get all log entries of a specific notification type.
+     *
+     * @param type - The notification type to filter by.
+     */
+    getByType(type: NotificationType): NotificationLogEntry[];
+    /**
+     * Get all log entries for a specific delivery channel.
+     *
+     * @param channel - The notification channel to filter by.
+     */
+    getByChannel(channel: NotificationChannel): NotificationLogEntry[];
+    /**
+     * Get all log entries within a date range (inclusive).
+     *
+     * @param startDate - The start of the date range.
+     * @param endDate - The end of the date range.
+     */
+    getByDateRange(startDate: Date, endDate: Date): NotificationLogEntry[];
+    /**
+     * Query notification history with combined filters.
+     *
+     * All query fields are optional. Omit a field to skip that filter.
+     * When multiple fields are provided, all conditions must match (AND logic).
+     *
+     * @param query - The filter criteria.
+     * @returns Matching log entries.
+     */
+    query(query: NotificationHistoryQuery): NotificationLogEntry[];
+    /**
+     * Query notification history with pagination.
+     *
+     * @param query - The filter criteria.
+     * @param page - The page number (1-indexed).
+     * @param pageSize - Number of items per page.
+     * @returns Paginated results.
+     */

+/**
+ * Order validators — pure validation functions for order operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,
+ * throwing descriptive errors when validation fails.
+ */
+import type { Order, OrderItem, OrderStatus } from '../models/order.js';
+/**
+ * Validate that an order exists.
+ *
+ * @param id - The order ID that was looked up.
+ * @param order - The result of the lookup (may be undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the order is not found.
+ */
+export declare function validateOrderExists(id: string, order: Order | undefined): asserts order is Order;
+/**
+ * Validate that an order has at least one item.
+ *
+ * @param items - The order items array.
+ * @throws Error if the items array is empty.
+ */
+export declare function validateOrderHasItems(items: OrderItem[]): void;
+/**
+ * Check whether a status transition is valid.
+ *
+ * Pure predicate — does not throw.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @returns true if the transition is allowed, false otherwise.
+ */
+export declare function isValidStatusTransition(from: OrderStatus, to: OrderStatus): boolean;
+/**
+ * Validate that a status transition is allowed.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @throws Error if the transition is not allowed.
+ */
+export declare function validateStatusTransition(from: OrderStatus, to: OrderStatus): void;

+/**
+ * Order validators — pure validation functions for order operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,
+ * throwing descriptive errors when validation fails.
+ */
+/**
+ * Allowed status transitions for orders.
+ *
+ * Each key maps to the list of statuses it can transition to.
+ */
+const STATUS_TRANSITIONS = {
+    pending: ['confirmed', 'cancelled'],
+    confirmed: ['shipped', 'cancelled'],
+    shipped: ['delivered'],
+    delivered: [],
+    cancelled: [],
+};
+/**
+ * Validate that an order exists.
+ *
+ * @param id - The order ID that was looked up.
+ * @param order - The result of the lookup (may be undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the order is not found.
+ */
+export function validateOrderExists(id, order) {
+    if (!order) {
+        throw new Error(`Order not found: ${id}`);
+    }
+}
+/**
+ * Validate that an order has at least one item.
+ *
+ * @param items - The order items array.
+ * @throws Error if the items array is empty.
+ */
+export function validateOrderHasItems(items) {
+    if (items.length === 0) {
+        throw new Error('Order must have at least one item');
+    }
+}
+/**
+ * Check whether a status transition is valid.
+ *
+ * Pure predicate — does not throw.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @returns true if the transition is allowed, false otherwise.
+ */
+export function isValidStatusTransition(from, to) {
+    return STATUS_TRANSITIONS[from].includes(to);
+}
+/**
+ * Validate that a status transition is allowed.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @throws Error if the transition is not allowed.
+ */
+export function validateStatusTransition(from, to) {
+    if (!isValidStatusTransition(from, to)) {
+        throw new Error(`Invalid status transition: ${from} -> ${to}`);
+    }
+}

+/**

+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,

+/**

+/**
+ * Validate that an email is not already taken.
+ *
+ * Used during user creation to enforce email uniqueness.
+ *
+ * @param email - The email to check.
+ * @param existingUser - A user already using that email (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the email is already in use.
+ */
+export declare function validateEmailNotTaken(email: string, existingUser: User | undefined): void;
+/**
+ * Validate that an email is available for a user update.
+ *
+ * Only checks uniqueness when the email is actually being changed.
+ *
+ * @param newEmail - The new email (may be undefined if not changing).
+ * @param currentEmail - The user's current email.
+ * @param existingUserWithEmail - A user already using the new email (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the new email is already in use by another user.
+ */
+export declare function validateEmailAvailableForUpdate(newEmail: string | undefined, currentEmail: string, existingUserWithEmail: User | undefined): void;

+/**

+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They receive already-fetched data and validate business rules,
+ * throwing descriptive errors when validation fails.
+ *
+ * Reusable across services (e.g. OrderService validates user existence

+/**
+ * Validate that a user exists.
+ *
+ * @param id - The user ID that was looked up.
+ * @param user - The result of the lookup (may be undefined).
+ * @throws Error if user is not found.
+ */
+export function validateUserExists(id, user) {
+    if (!user) {
+        throw new Error(`User not found: ${id}`);
+    }
+}
+/**
+ * Validate that an email is not already taken.
+ *
+ * Used during user creation to enforce email uniqueness.
+ *
+ * @param email - The email to check.
+ * @param existingUser - A user already using that email (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the email is already in use.
+ */
+export function validateEmailNotTaken(email, existingUser) {
+    if (existingUser) {
+        throw new Error(`User with email ${email} already exists`);
+    }
+}
+/**
+ * Validate that an email is available for a user update.
+ *
+ * Only checks uniqueness when the email is actually being changed.
+ *
+ * @param newEmail - The new email (may be undefined if not changing).
+ * @param currentEmail - The user's current email.
+ * @param existingUserWithEmail - A user already using the new email (or undefined).
+ * @throws Error if the new email is already in use by another user.
+ */
+export function validateEmailAvailableForUpdate(newEmail, currentEmail, existingUserWithEmail) {
+    if (newEmail && newEmail !== currentEmail && existingUserWithEmail) {
+        throw new Error(`Email ${newEmail} is already in use`);
+    }

# SMS Channel — Implementation Notes

**Date:** 2026-03-27
**Status:** COMPLETE — SMS adapter implemented AND user preferences updated for multi-channel support

---

## What Was Done (Session 8)

1. **Created `SmsAdapter`** (`src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`)
   - Implements the `NotificationAdapter` interface (same pattern as `EmailAdapter` and `WebhookAdapter`)
   - Phone number validation (E.164 and domestic formats)
   - SMS message formatting with character-aware truncation (160-char default)
   - Stub mode for development/testing; production mode placeholder with retry logic
   - Exported from `src/index.ts`

2. **Tests added** (`tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts`)
   - Full test coverage for send, validation, formatting, configuration, and edge cases

3. **Registered in public API** (`src/index.ts`)
   - `SmsAdapter`, `defaultSmsConfig`, `isValidPhoneNumber`, `resolvePhoneNumber`, `formatSmsMessage`
   - Types: `SmsAdapterConfig`, `SmsMessage`

---

## Multi-Channel Preferences Update (Session 9)

### Decision Change

> **Old assumption (Session 3):** "Currently focused on email settings since email is the only notification channel."
>
> **New reality (Session 8+9):** Email, SMS, and Webhook are all supported channels. The preferences model now supports per-channel settings.

### Changes Made

| # | File | Change |
|---|------|--------|
| 1 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Added `SmsPreferences` interface |
| 2 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Added `WebhookPreferences` interface |
| 3 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Added `sms` and `webhook` fields to `UserNotificationPreferences` |
| 4 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Updated `CreatePreferencesInput` with optional `sms` and `webhook` fields |
| 5 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Updated `UpdatePreferencesInput` with optional `sms` and `webhook` fields |
| 6 | `src/models/user-preferences.ts` | Added `getDefaultSmsPreferences()` and `getDefaultWebhookPreferences()` functions |
| 7 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Added `shouldSendSms()` private method |
| 8 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Added `shouldSendWebhook()` private method |
| 9 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Updated `shouldSendNotification()` to handle `sms` and `webhook` channels |
| 10 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Updated `createPreferences()` to handle `sms` and `webhook` fields |
| 11 | `src/services/preferences.service.ts` | Updated `updatePreferences()` to handle `sms` and `webhook` fields |
| 12 | `src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts` | Updated `toDomain()` and `toRecord()` for SMS and webhook fields |
| 13 | `src/index.ts` | Exported new types (`SmsPreferences`, `WebhookPreferences`) and functions |
| 14 | `tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts` | Added comprehensive multi-channel tests |
| 15 | `tests/validators/preferences.validator.test.ts` | Updated helper to include `sms` and `webhook` fields |

### Default Preferences

- **Email:** Enabled by default, immediate frequency, all categories enabled
- **SMS:** Enabled by default, all categories enabled (always immediate)
- **Webhook:** Disabled by default (requires URL configuration), all categories enabled

### Backward Compatibility

- The `email` field on `UserNotificationPreferences` is unchanged in structure
- `CreatePreferencesInput` and `UpdatePreferencesInput` still accept `email`-only input — `sms` and `webhook` fields are optional and default to sensible values
- The `NotificationService` continues to work without a `PreferencesService` (all notifications sent)
- Existing code that only uses email preferences continues to work without modification

---

## How to Register Adapters

```typescript
import {
  EventBus,
  NotificationService,
  PreferencesService,
  PreferencesRepository,
  Database,
  EmailAdapter,
  SmsAdapter,
  WebhookAdapter,
} from './src/index.js';

const eventBus = new EventBus();
const notificationService = new NotificationService(eventBus);

// Register adapters for each channel
notificationService.registerAdapter(new EmailAdapter({ stubMode: true }));
notificationService.registerAdapter(new SmsAdapter({ stubMode: true }));
notificationService.registerAdapter(new WebhookAdapter({
  url: 'https://hooks.example.com',
  stubMode: true,
}));

// Wire up preferences service for per-channel opt-out
const db = new Database();
const prefsRepo = new PreferencesRepository(db);
const prefsService = new PreferencesService(prefsRepo);
notificationService.setPreferencesService(prefsService);
```

The notification service will check per-channel preferences before dispatching to each adapter.
